observations.recommendation:
We recommended and
Management agreed to instruct the
–
a) Municipal Accounting Office
to (i) coordinate with the
Municipal Treasurer’s Office
(MTO) for the reconciliation of
their records; (ii)
prepare/update subsidiary
ledgers for RPT and SET
Receivable accounts; (iii)
check the journal entries made
in prior years; and (iv) prepare
adjusting entries, as warranted;
and
b) MTO in coordination with the
Sanggunian, to exhaust all
remedies to decrease tax
delinquencies such as but not
limited to: (i) posting of notice
of delinquency at the main
entrance of the municipal hall
and in a publicly accessible and
conspicuous place in each
barangay concerned as required
by the Local Government
Code; and (ii) if possible,
formally request the assistance
of the Punong Barangays in
identifying the locations of the
delinquent taxpayers so that
notice of tax delinquency can
be served accordingly, which
may eventually increase tax
collections beneficial both for the
Municipality and barangays.
observations.managementAction:
See Observation No.
2, Part II of the AAR
for CY 2024.
observations.reason: See Observation No. 2, Part II of the AAR for CY 2024.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and
Management agreed to –
a. reconstitute the Inventory
Committee to address the need
to conduct a physical count of
all inventory to establish the
correct balances of the
inventory accounts and to be
able to prepare and submit the
Report on the Physical Count
of Inventories (RCPI);
b. direct the General Service
Office to:
 maintain Stock Card for each
commodity/stock for
reconciliation with Supplies
Ledger Card of the Accounting
Office;
 in coordination with MHO and
MSWD to prepare the
Inspection and Acceptance
Report of all the donated
supplies/ inventories received
by the Municipality and submit
it together with a copy of the
Property Transfer Receipt
(PTR) to the Accounting Office
for recording; and
 observe Sec. 121, Chapter 7,
Volume I of MNGAS for
LGUs in the issuance of
supplies.
c. direct the Accountant to:
 observe the Perpetual
Inventory Method of
accounting to timely record the
distributions/ issuances of
supplies/inventories to end-
users;
 abide by the provisions of the
MNGAS on the use of the
Moving Average Method of
costing the inventory;
 in coordination with GSO to
record all the donated
supplies/inventories;
 maintain Supplies Ledger
Cards (SLCs) for each
commodity/stock to be
reconciled with Stock Cards
(SCs) of the GSO;
 record the distributions/
issuances of supplies based on
the Summary of Supplies and
Materials Issued (SSMI) and
other supporting documents
that would warrant the
accuracy of the entry made; and
 make an adjusting entry, based
on valid supporting documents,
to exclude the distributed relief See Observation No.
goods from the balance of 4, Part II of the AAR
Inventory Accounts to reflect for CY 2024 for the
the accurate balance of the updated audit
inventory. observations.
observations.reason: See Observation No. 4, Part II of the AAR for CY 2024 for the updated audit observations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and
Management agreed to –
a) direct the Municipal
Accountant to (i) ensure the
proper use of Construction in
Progress account and
Construction Materials
Inventory account, and (ii)
expedite the completion of
PPELC;
b) instruct the GSO to (i)
complete the Property Cards
for all PPEs; and (ii) take
necessary actions to identify all
unserviceable assets and
prepare the Inventory and
Inspection Report of
Unserviceable Property so that
proper disposal thereof could
be made;
c) instruct the Inventory
Committee to (i) conduct
annual physical count of all
PPE and upon completion of
the count, reconcile the result
of actual count with the
existing inventory records of
the accounting office and GSO;
(ii) consequently prepare the
RPCPPE in prescribed format
and submit the same to the
Office of the Auditor on or
before January 31 of the
following year; and (iii)
consider conducting one-time
cleansing of PPE accounts in
accordance with COA Circular
No. 2020-006; and
d) instruct the Appraisal and
Disposal Committee to
facilitate disposal of See Observation Nos.
unserviceable assets pursuant 5-8, Part II of the AAR
to Section 79 of PD No. 1445. for CY 2024 for the
updated audit
observations.
observations.reason: See Observation Nos. 5-8, Part II of the AAR for CY 2024 for the updated audit observations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and
Management agreed to –
a) direct all personnel concerned
to prepare APP and PPMP in
accordance with Section 7 of
IRR of R.A. No. 9184;
b) require the BAC Secretariat to
submit to the GPPB and copy
furnish the Audit Team the
revised APP and PPMP for CY
2023 and the year thereafter;
c) instruct the BAC Secretariat to
post all the awarded contract’s
Notice of Award (NOA) and
Notice to Proceed (NTP) on the
Municipality’s website; and
d) direct the BAC to prepare the
Procurement Monitoring
Report (PMR) within 14
calendar days after the end of
each semester and submit it to
GPBB, copy furnish the Audit
Team.

We recommended and
Management agreed to strictly
adhere with the guidelines under
COA Circular No. 2012-001 on the
granting of cash advance and its
liquidation through:
i. Instructing the Municipal
Accountant, the Municipal
Budget Officer, and the
Municipal Treasurer to refrain
from processing disbursement
vouchers of, approving
obligations to, and preparing
checks for, a new cash advance
of an employee until his/her
previous cash advance has been
settled, respectively and refrain
from granting cash advances
that are above the approved
maximum cash accountability
of accountable officer’s fidelity
bond;
ii. Designating additional
disbursing officers for cash
advance to prevent granting of
multiple cash advances to one
disbursing officer only; and
iii. Directing the disbursing
officers to strictly liquidate
cash advances granted to them
within the prescribed timeline.
observations.reason: See Observation No. 3, Part II of the AAR for CY 2024 for the updated audit observations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and
Management agreed to direct the –
a. General Services Officer to (i)
review the Report on the
Physical Count of Property,
Plant, and Equipment
(RPCPPE) for CY 2023 or
equivalent documents, to
ascertain that all insurable
government properties
physically identified are
insured with the GSIS; and (ii)
submit the Property Inventory
Form (PIF) to the Audit Team
not later than April 30 of each
year; and
b. Municipal Accountant to
record prepayments initially as
Assets and subsequently
recognize Expenses upon
consumption, pursuant to
Section 64, Chapter 3, Volume
I of the Manual on the New
Government Accounting
System (MNGAS) for Local
Government Units (LGUs)
using the appropriate account
titles prescribed in the Revised
Chart of Accounts for LGUs.

We recommended and
Management agreed to require the
Municipal Engineer and other
concerned officials to provide the
Audit Team at the beginning of the
year with the list of all ongoing
projects/programs/activities (PPA)
and those that are to be
implemented during the year with
the required details. Submit also to
the Audit Team information and
evidence that appropriate
signboards and/or public notices
were already posted 10 days after
the award of the infrastructure
project or before the start of PPA.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to
direct the Municipal Engineer to expedite the application for the Water Permit needed in the implementation of the Level III Potable Water Supply System in Barangay Mabiga and instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to be informed from time to time about the latest Local Budget Circular related to the transfer of funds received by the Municipality to ensure its proper implementation specifically the posting and reporting requirements.

We recommended and
Management agreed to instruct the
Municipal Accountant or
authorized representative to –
a. submit complete supporting
documents of the grant of cash
advances for Confidential Fund
(CF) within the prescribed
period, pursuant to Item 6.1 of
COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND
Joint Circular 2015-01 dated
January 8, 2015;
b. ensure that all cash advances
for CF covered by the current
Joint Circular shall be
liquidated at year-end; and
c. communicate with the COA-
ICFAO on the updated status of
the liquidation of the long-
outstanding cash advances for
CF totaling ₱2,400,000.00,
follow-up the issuance of credit
advice if found in order,
otherwise comply with the
deficiencies noted by the COA-
ICFAO in coordination with
the Accountable Officer.

We recommended and the Local
Chief Executive agreed to instruct
the GFPS to (i) submit the GAD
Plan and Budget to the Audit Team
within the prescribed period for
proper evaluation thereof; (ii)
inform the concerned DILG Office
in writing if there will be changes
in the DILG-endorsed GPB; and
(iii) ensure that the supporting
documents used to accomplish
GAD AR were adequate to
validate the recorded actual
results.

We recommended and
Management agreed to instruct the
–
a) Municipal Treasurer’s Office
(MTO) and Business Permit
and Licensing Office to (i)
remind the businesses/ Implemented
taxpayers on the submission of
certified copies of their income
tax returns (ITR) on or before
April 30 of each year as
required by the Municipality’s
Revenue Code; and (ii) if Implemented
possible, request from the
Bureau of Internal Revenue a
list of all registered business
establishments in Hermosa,
Bataan with their declared
gross sales for CY 2022 copy
furnish the Audit Team;
b) MTO to conduct the Not Implemented
examination of books of
accounts and pertinent records
pursuant to Section 171 of the
Local Government Code
(LGC); and
c) Municipal Accountant to (i)
prepare subsidiary ledger for Not Implemented
Business Tax account; and (ii)
utilize the Revised Chart of Implemented
Accounts for LGUs in
recording the collection of See Observation No.
business tax and other 11, Part II of the AAR
fees/charges. for CY 2024 for the
updated audit
observations.
observations.reason: See Observation No. 11, Part II of the AAR for CY 2024 for the updated audit observations.

observations.recommendation:
We also recommended and
Management agreed to instruct the
Municipal Accountant to see to it
that all remittances are made
within the deadlines set by the
BIR.

We recommended and
Management agreed to instruct the
Municipal Accountant to prepare
subsidiary ledgers for the Due to
GSIS account and submit the same
together with the corresponding
proof of remittance of the ending
balance based on the reconciliation
made.

We recommended and
Management agreed to instruct the
Municipal Accountant to prepare
subsidiary ledgers for the Due to
PAG-IBIG account and submit the
same together with the
corresponding proof of remittance
of the ending balance based on the
reconciliation made.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the
necessary adjusting entries:
Account Title Account Number Debit Credit
Real Property Tax Receivable 1-03-01-020 ₱7,926,784.87
Deferred Real Property Tax 2-05-01-010 ₱7,926,784.87
Account Title Account Number Debit Credit
Special Education Tax Receivable 1-03-01-030 ₱7,926,784.87
Deferred Special Education Tax 2-05-01-020 ₱7,926,784.87

We recommended and Management agreed to designate additional SDOs to
eliminate granting of additional cash advances to disbursing officers with
unliquidated cash advances.

We recommended and Management agreed that the:
a. Inventory Committee immediately conduct a physical inventory of both
donated and purchased medicines and medical supplies and submit the RPCI to the accounting office for reconciliation;
b. Accounting Office conduct reconciliation with MHO’s inventory records
and draw adjusting entries to reflect the accurate balance of inventory;
c. MHO implement proper recording procedures for inventory transactions,
ensure that supporting documents are maintained for all issuances, and
coordinate with the Accounting Office regarding reporting requirements;
d. GSO, provide complete documentation of procured medicines and medical
supplies transferred to MHO for recording in the eLMIS;
e. Accounting Office, GSO, and MHO design and implement control
procedures to ensure the recording of donated inventories; and
f. Accounting Office and GSO/Distribution Center (Municipal Health
Office) maintain SLC and SC, respectively.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to make an adjusting
journal entry to accurately reflect the construction of the storage room.

We recommended and the Inventory Committee agreed to prepare and
implement an inventory-taking plan clearly defining the activities to be undertaken,
personnel responsible, timelines, identified risks and corresponding risk mitigation
strategies.

We recommended and the GSO and Accounting Office agreed to expedite the
completion of their property records to strengthen the agency’s internal control over
its property.

We recommended and Management agreed that the:


GSO cause the immediate preparation of IIRUP and IIRUSP and submission
thereof to the Disposal Committee;

Disposal Committee facilitate the speedy disposal of unserviceable properties
upon receipt of IIRUP; and

Accountant update the financial records upon disposal.

We recommended and Management agreed that the –
a. Human Resource Management Officer provide measurement on the earned
leave credits using Section 3.1 of DBM-BC No. 2016-2 dated March 29, 2016,
and forward the same to Municipal Accountant;
b. Municipal Accountant (i) accrue the earned leaves using appropriate accounts,
and (ii) disclose information related to employee leave benefits; and
c. Municipal Budget Officer use TLB instead of OPB for monetization of leave
credits for consistency of reports.

We recommended and Management agreed to (i) institute control measures to
ensure submission of ITRs of business entities, and (ii) instruct the Municipal
Treasurer or authorized representatives to examine the books of accounts of
businesses, prioritizing those that consistently fail to submit ITRs or have
discrepancies between their declared gross sales/receipts and the amounts provided
by the BIR.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to a) enhance the
completeness and compliance of the Notes to the Financial Statements by utilizing a
standardized disclosure checklist, serving as a guide to ensure that all required
disclosures are accurately and consistently presented in accordance with IPSAS; and
b) adjust material prior period errors retrospectively in accordance with paragraph
47 of IPSAS 3.

We recommended and Management agreed that (a) the Head of the HRMO
develop policy guidelines for the hiring/rehiring of JO personnel defining attendance
monitoring, supervision, pay rates determination and performance assessment; (b)
clearly define specific job descriptions and functions in the employment contract; and
(c) ensure that the hiring of JO personnel is supported with a SB resolution.

We recommended and the GAD Focal Point System agreed to undergo training
on gender-analysis and application of the HGDG tool and ensure its application in
GAD planning and accomplishment reporting.

We recommended and Management agreed to maintain its compliance with the
last paragraph of Section 21 of RA No. 10121, and Items 5.1.10 and 5.1.13 of COA
Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended and Management agreed to (i) allocate sufficient funding for
the MDRRMO’s MOOE and CO requirements; and (ii) utilize the 5% LDRRMF
strictly for projects and activities exclusively related to disaster prevention,
mitigation, preparedness, response and rehabilitation and recovery.

We recommended and Management agreed to continuously ensure the
optimal utilization of the 20% DF in accordance with the prescribed guidelines and the
exclusion of expenditure items under Item 3.2.5 of the JMC.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that all remittances are
made within the deadlines set by the BIR.

We recommended and Management agreed to (i) identify and reconcile the
remaining balance and remit the same to the GSIS; and (ii) ensure that all remittances
are made within the deadlines set by the GSIS

We recommended and Management agreed to (i) identify and reconcile the
remaining balance and remit the same to the Pag-IBIG; and (ii) ensure that all
remittances are made within the deadlines set by the Pag-IBIG.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that all remittances are
made within the deadlines set by the PhilHealth.

We recommended and Management agreed to remain compliant with laws, accounting,
budgeting, and auditing rules and regulations to prevent audit suspension, disallowance
and charges.

We recommended and
Management agreed to direct the –
a. Municipal Treasurer to (i)
furnish Schedule of Unreleased
Checks as of year-end to the
Municipal Accountant; and (ii)
properly accomplish the
required details in the Check
Register;
b. Municipal Accountant to
prepare journal entry to revert
the cash balance of unreleased
checks at year-end and the
reversing entry in the ensuing
year; and
c. proper coordination between
Treasury and Accounting
Offices to monitor the actual
status of issued checks so that
appropriate adjustments could
be made promptly.

We recommended and
Management agreed to –
a. Discontinue the practice of
making payment of
transactions on a
reimbursement basis to avoid
future audit disallowance;
b. Direct the accountant to initiate
the establishment of additional
petty cash fund in relation to
emergency purchases that are
from time to time paid through
reimbursements; and
c. Instruct the accountant, the
budget officer, and the
treasurer to exercise direct
payment to suppliers through
the issuance of checks for large
purchases of goods and
services and by observing the
proper mode of procurement.

We recommended and
Management agreed to require the
BAC Secretariat and General
Services Office to submit all
copies of perfected contracts with
all the required supporting
documents within five days from
perfection and all documents
pertaining to delivery and
acceptance of procured items
within 24 hours upon delivery.

We recommended and
Management agreed to direct the –
a. Municipal Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management
Officer (MDRRMO) to include
the unexpended balance of the
LDRRMF in prior years in the
LDRRMF Investment Plan;
and
b. Municipal Accountant to (i)
refrain from transferring the
unexpended balance of the
LDRRMF- Capital Outlay
from the General Fund to the
Special Trust Fund; (ii) in
coordination with the
MDRRMO, utilize any savings
therefrom for LGU’s disaster
risk reduction and management
programs; and (iii) submit the
Report on Sources and
Utilization of LDRRMF to the
Audit Team on or before 15th
day after the end of each month.

We recommended and
Management agreed to (i) ensure
that the development projects to be
funded out of the 20%
Development Fund (DF) are well-
planned and procurement-and-
implementation-ready; and (ii)
ensure that the 20% DF shall be
utilized only for the priority
development projects embodied in
the Municipality’s Comprehensive
Development Plan, pursuant to
Items 3.2.1 and 3.2.3 of DBM-
DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated
November 4, 2020.

We recommended and the LCE
agreed to instruct the concerned
Municipal officials to:
a. submit the lacking documents
in compliance with item 13.1 of
COA Circular No. 2012-001
dated June 14, 2012, for further
evaluation as a basis for the
Audit Team’s subsequent audit
action;
b. submit the Certified photocopy
of the photocopied documents
under para.1.3, items h to j;
c. submit a written explanation on
the noted deficiencies;
d. cause the proper titling of the
purchased lot in the name of the
Municipality as required by
Section 23 (2), Chapter 4, Title
1-B, Book V, of the 1987
Administrative Code; and
e. on future similar undertakings,
direct the Accountant to check
the completeness of the
supporting documents before
payment as required by COA
Circular no. 2012-001.

We recommended and
Management agreed to instruct the
Municipal Accountant to prepare
subsidiary ledgers for the Due to
PhilHealth account and submit the
same together with the
corresponding proof of remittance
of the ending balance based on the
reconciliation made.

We recommended and Implemented
Management agreed to remain
compliant with laws, accounting,
budgeting, and auditing rules and
regulations to prevent audit
suspension, disallowance and
charges.

observations.reason:
The Accounting
Office is in the
process of
completing the
PPELCs.

observations.recommendation:
We also reiterated prior year’s recommendation and Management agreed to –
a) instruct the GSO to (i) prepare and maintain Property Cards for all PPEs; and (ii) take necessary actions to identify all unserviceable assets and prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property so that proper disposal thereof could be made;
observations.reason:
The GSO is also in
the process of
completing the
Property Cards.

We also reiterated prior year’s recommendation and Management agreed to –
a) instruct the GSO to (i) prepare and maintain Property Cards for all PPEs; and (ii) take necessary actions to identify all unserviceable assets and prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property so that proper disposal thereof could be made;
observations.reason:
Inventory and
Inspection Report
of Unserviceable
Property was not

b) instruct the Inventory Committee to (i) conduct annual physical count of all PPE and upon completion of the count, reconcile the result of actual count with the existing inventory records of the accounting office and GSO; (ii) consequently prepare the RPCPPE in prescribed format and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor on or before January 31 of the following year; and (iii) consider conducting one-time cleansing of PPE accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006; and
observations.reason:
The Inventory
Committee did
not conduct the
required physical
count of PPE
which resulted in

of the RPCPPE.

observations.recommendation:
We also reiterated prior year’s recommendation and Management agreed to –
c) instruct the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to facilitate disposal of unserviceable assets pursuant to Section 79 of PD No. 1445.
observations.reason:
Numerous
unserviceable and
obsolete assets
owned by the
Municipality
remained
undisposed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to -
a. xxx; and
b. direct the Municipal Accountant to (i) prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers (SLs) to support of the RPT and SET accounts in order to have proper reconciliation with the MTO accounts regularly; and (ii) make necessary adjustment to reflect the correct amount of RPT/SET Receivable.
observations.reason:
The preparation of
SLs for RPT and
SET Receivable
accounts is on-
going.

observations.recommendation: Moreover, we also reiterated prior year’s recommendation for Management to require the Municipal Treasurer to (i) implement procedures and strategies on how to further improve collection efficiency, particularly on delinquent taxpayers; (ii) xxx; and (iii) include remarks/statement regarding the reason/s for non-collection or non-payment in the certified list of all real property tax delinquencies which remained uncollected or unpaid for at least one year to the request for assistance in the enforcement of the remedies for collection submitted to the Sanggunian.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation and Management agreed that the Municipality, through its GFPS, to (a) submit the GPB to the Audit Team within the prescribed period for proper evaluation thereof; (b) inform the concerned DILG Office in writing if there will be changes in the DILG-endorsed GPB; and (c) xxx.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the –
a. Municipal Treasurer to (i) furnish Schedule of Unreleased Checks as of year-
end to the Municipal Accountant; and (ii) properly accomplish the required
details in the Check Register;
b. Municipal Accountant to prepare journal entry to revert the cash balance of
unreleased checks at year-end and the reversing entry in the ensuing year; and
c. proper coordination between Treasury and Accounting Offices to monitor the
actual status of issued checks so that appropriate adjustments could be made
promptly.

We recommended and Management agreed to –
a) direct all personnel concerned to prepare APP and PPMP in accordance
with Section 7 of IRR of R.A. No. 9184;
b) require the BAC Secretariat to submit to the GPPB and copy furnish the
Audit Team the revised APP and PPMP for CY 2023 and the year
thereafter;
c) instruct the BAC Secretariat to post all the awarded contract’s Notice of
Award (NOA) and Notice to Proceed (NTP) on the Municipality’s website;
and
d) direct the BAC to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR)
within 14 calendar days after the end of each semester and submit it to
GPBB, copy furnish the Audit Team.
a. instruct the Inventory Committee to i. conduct annual physical count of PPE and upon completion of the physical count, reconcile the result of actual count with the existing inventory records;

b. direct the Municipal Accountant to iv. upon receipt of list of unserviceable assets, include disclosures relating to unserviceable and/or held for disposal assets in the Notes to Financial Statements under ‘Property, Plant, and Equipment’; and

b. direct the Municipal Accountant to v. effect the eventual derecognition/dropping from the books of the disposed properties based on required supporting documents for proper presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

observations.reason: Not Implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to –
a) direct the Municipal Accountant to –
➢ prepare necessary adjusting entries for reclassification of small
tangible items with estimated useful life of more than one year and with
amount valued below ₱15,000.00 to respective expense accounts, and
start recording similar purchases as inventories upon acquisition and
as expense upon issuance;
➢ ensure the correctness of amount recorded as Construction in Progress;
➢ see to it that only those expenditures incurred are obligated; and
➢ prepare and maintain PPELC.

We also reiterated prior year’s recommendation and Management agreed to –
a) instruct the GSO to (i) prepare and maintain Property Cards for all PPEs; and
(ii) take necessary actions to identify all unserviceable assets and prepare the
Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property so that proper
disposal thereof could be made;
b) instruct the Inventory Committee to (i) conduct annual physical count of all
PPE and upon completion of the count, reconcile the result of actual count
with the existing inventory records of the accounting office and GSO; (ii)
consequently prepare the RPCPPE in prescribed format and submit the same
to the Office of the Auditor on or before January 31 of the following year; and
(iii) consider conducting one-time cleansing of PPE accounts in accordance
with COA Circular No. 2020-006; and
c) instruct the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to facilitate disposal of
unserviceable assets pursuant to Section 79 of PD 1445.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant to (i) revert the accounts payable with inadequate documentation and not
covered by perfected contracts on record and funded out of General Fund and Special
Education Fund to unappropriated surplus; and (ii) ensure the propriety of the
documentation for Accounts Payable before recoding the same.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive
instruct the Bids and Awards Committee and the Implementing Offices to properly
plan and schedule all procurement activities pertaining to development projects to
pave way for their timely implementation and completion.

We recommended that the -
a.) Municipality continuously implement programs, projects and activities that
will aid and protect its constituents from the adverse effects of pandemic;
b.) Management advise the BAC to comply with the posting requirements of
procurement related to COVID-19 as requisite by GPPB Circular No. 02-2021
dated March 25, 2021 and RA 11525 or the Vaccination Program Act of 2021; and
c.) Management thru the Accounting Department ascertain that transactions are
supported with complete documentation in accordance with Section 4(6) of PD
1445.

We recommended and Management agreed thru the Municipal Accounting
Office and lead Departments to ensure the completeness of supporting documents as
required by the Memoranda of Agreement and Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.

We recommended and Management agreed to -
a. instruct the Municipal Assessor to complete the tax mapping of the five
remaining barangays so that proper valuation of real properties be made; and
b. direct the Municipal Accountant to (i) prepare and maintain subsidiary
ledgers (SLs) to support of the RPT and SET accounts in order to have proper
reconciliation with the MTO regularly; and (ii) make necessary adjustment to
reflect the correct amount of RPT/SET Receivable.

Moreover, we also reiterated prior year’s recommendation for Management
to require the Municipal Treasurer to (i) implement procedures and strategies on how
to further improve collection efficiency, particularly on delinquent tax payers; (ii)
publish yearly the notice of delinquency once a week for two consecutive weeks in a
newspaper of general circulation; and (iii) include remarks/statement regarding the
reason/s for non-collection or non-payment in the certified list of all real property tax
delinquencies which remained uncollected or unpaid for at least one year to the request
for assistance in the enforcement of the remedies for collection submitted to the
Sanggunian.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation and Management
agreed that the Municipality, through its GFPS, to (a) submit the GPB to the Audit
Team within the prescribed period for proper evaluation thereof; (b) inform the
concerned DILG Office in writing if there will be changes in the DILG-endorsed
GPB; and (c) ensure that the PPAs are in alignment with the GAD objectives of truly
addressing gender issues towards gender equality and women empowerment.

We also recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal
Accountant to see to it that all remittances are made within the deadlines set by the
BIR.

Accountant to prepare subsidiary ledgers for the Due to GSIS account and submit
the same together with the corresponding proof of remittance of the ending balance
based on the reconciliation made.

Accountant to prepare subsidiary ledgers for the Due to PAG-IBIG account and
submit the same together with the corresponding proof of remittance of the ending
balance based on the reconciliation made.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal

submit the same together with the corresponding proof of remittance of the ending
balance based on the reconciliation made.

We recommended and Management agreed to remain compliant with laws,
accounting, budgeting, and auditing rules and regulations to prevent audit
suspension, disallowance and charges.

observations.recommendation: a) instruct the Municipal Treasurer to communicate with the Office of the Provincial Assessor and/or Office of the Provincial Treasurer, thru official letter, regarding the system linkage of the Assessor’s Office and the Municipal Treasurer’s Office in order to attain full utilization of the system as intended thus, service provider conducted an on-site visit to the municipalities of Bataan and conducted the following activities:
observations.managementAction:
The MTO had already communicated May 26, 2022 regarding the matter. Also, the PATAS was not fully utilized by the MTO as intended.
In GEODATA – Comm., the service provider conducted an on-site visit to the municipalities of Bataan and conducted the following activities:
1. Check the connection of MTO the server of PATAS
2. Re-install the PATAS system to the workstations of the user.
3. Set-up the subscription synchronization of the Treasury data-base.
4. To set-up publisher synchronization to Capitol server.
5. Gather remaining concerns of Assessor and Treasury in the system.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to –
a. communicate with the ICFAU regarding the status of submitted Intelligence
Fund liquidation reports totaling ₱2,500,000.00 which were not covered by the
current JC;
b. instruct the Municipal Accountant to (i) ensure the completeness of the
documents before granting the cash advance for CF; and (ii) submit to the Audit
Team Leader the Disbursement Vouchers for the cash advance of confidential fund
along with the complete supporting documents within seven days from the release of
the check on a sealed envelope and properly labeled as “Confidential – for COA
ATL only”; and
c. strictly adhere with the guidelines under COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint
Circular 2015-01 on the granting, recording and reporting of confidential fund.

We recommended and Management agreed to -
a) reorganize its Inventory Committee and require them to –
• conduct complete annual physical count of PPE;
• reconcile the actual count and the existing inventory records, if there is any
and submit complete and duly signed RPCPPE to the Office of the Auditor
not later than the prescribed deadline by the Manual;
• identify the reasons for the differences noted before making any
adjustment;
b) reorganize its Disposal and Appraisal Committee and instruct them to –
• inspect unserviceable equipment and property to verify justification for
disposal;
• set the final appraised value of all disposable properties;
• recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) for approval, the manner
of disposal; and
• conduct public bidding for the sale of disposable property on an ‘as is,
where is basis’ and recommend corresponding award thereof.
c) instruct the GSO to adhere with the guidelines on the use of government
vehicles in which vehicles to bear red plates and marking of “For Official Use Only”;
and
d) revise Section II (5) of EO No. 17 that the ‘conduct of physical inventory shall
be properly coordinated with the COA Audit Team’.
3.23 We reiterated the following recommendations and Management agreed to –
a) Direct the OIC-GSO to (i) cause the preparation and maintenance of
complete and updated Property Cards to enable reconciliation with the general
ledger balances and the inventory report and (ii) take the necessary action to
identify all unserviceable government properties and prepare the Inventory and
47
Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) so that proper disposal could
be undertaken; and
b) direct the Municipal Accountant to (i) set up and maintain PPE and Real
Property Ledger Cards; and (ii) make the necessary adjusting entry for properties
disposed of.

We recommended and Management agreed to –
a) consider the application of postpaid plans under a corporate/enterprise
account of the LGU to a chosen telecommunication service provider for economical
reasons, to foster equity in the grant of allowances in relation to actual communication
needs of the entity and for ease in monitoring and control of mobile expenses;
b) thru the assistance of the SB, establish clear-cut guidelines for the grant of
communication allowances to officials and employees; and
c) instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the required supporting
documents for mobile allowance are attached to the DV to support the validity of
the claims.

We recommended and the Management agreed to –

(c) monitor the compliance of LGU and Econest with the provisions included in the MOA regarding the operations of the SLF.
observations.managementAction: The Management has drafted addendum on the MOA with Econest.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in Observation No. 8

There was still no finalization on the addendum on the terms and agreement of the LGU with Econest.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that through its GFPS – 
 
(d) continuously comply with the mechanisms and processes in accordance with the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 or Guidelines on the Localization of the Magna Carta of Women.
observations.reason: In CY 2019, some deviations/non-implementation of provisions of Municipality’s GAD Code of 2017 were noted.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated the following recommendations and the Management agreed that:

Inventory Committee to conduct an annual comprehensive/ complete physical inventory of PPE by description type and submit a report thereon to the Office of the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year;
 
OIC-GSO to take the necessary action to identify all unserviceable government properties and prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) so that proper disposal could be undertaken and the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting entry for properties already disposed.

Municipal Accountant and GSO to cause the preparation and maintenance of complete and updated PPE/Real Property Ledger Cards/ Property Cards to enable reconciliation with the general ledger balances and the inventory report, and monitor improvement and disposition of each property.

Prepare subsidiary ledgers for the Land account with complete details, reconcile the inventory list of the land from the accounting and the treasury; and provide budget and cause the transfer of land titles of all land acquired through donation and annotation of land without certificate of title to the Registry of Deeds.
observations.managementAction: The General Services Office (GSO) in the LGU of Hermosa was only created in 2014. Individual property ledger/cards were not yet prepared and maintained owing to change in administration every election.
observations.reason:
Lack of GSO personnel to complete the conduct of physical count and preparation of required reports on the recording and disposal of properties.

Reiterated in Observation No. 1

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the:
Inventory Committee to conduct complete annual physical count of PPEs and inventories and submit RPCPPE and RCPI, respectively, to the Office of the Auditor not later than the prescribed deadline by the Manual;
Accounting Office set up and maintain PPE and Real Property Ledger Cards and the GSO cause the preparation and maintenance of complete and updated Property Cards to enable reconciliation with the general ledger balances and the inventory report; and
OIC-GSO take the necessary action to identify all unserviceable government properties and prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) so that proper disposal could be undertaken, and Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting entry for properties that were already disposed.

We recommended and the Management agreed that upon completion of the general revision of property assessments, management provide additional personnel to finish updating the Certified List of Taxpayers for the Accountant to take up in the books, the RPT/SET Receivable with the corresponding subsidiary ledgers (SL) to monitor tax collections and delinquencies. 

We also recommended and the Management agreed to make representations with the Provincial Assessor’s Office to address the problems encountered on the newly installed Property Assessment and Tax Administration System (PATAS) and to finally link the system with the Municipal Treasurer’s Office.
observations.managementAction: Updating of the Certified List of Taxpayers with corresponding due and collectible is not yet possible because of the ongoing general revision of property assessments and classification at the Municipal Assessor’s Office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed that the OIC- Municipal Accountant

 (a) send demand letters to the concerned accountable officers for the liquidation of their accountabilities; and

(b) require the concerned personnel to liquidate their cash advances as soon as the purpose thereof had been accomplished.
observations.managementAction:
Management are seeking for the recommendation /approval from the Commission on Audit if cash advances made by DepED personnel as well as the cash advance made by the late Municipal Treasurer Rafael Aguilar may be written-off due to aging and possibility of collection is remote.

Requirements per Audit Observation Memoranda issued in October 2017 by the Intelligence/ Confidential Fund Audit Office (ICFAO) pertaining to Confidential Fund of the Former LCEs are being complied with and yet to be submitted to the Commission on Audit, Central Office. 

Management commit themselves to strictly comply and adhere to the limitations on the granting and liquidation of cash advances as recommended.
observations.reason:
Partially Implemented

Efforts to collect the unliquidated cash advances of DepED personnel were futile because some of them have retired from the service and moved to other countries and one passed away.

The advances for payroll made by the late Municipal Treasurer Rafael Aguilar in the year 1982 remained unliquidated in the Municipality’s books. Upon inquiry/ interview from officers and employees who know him, Mr. Aguilar was a victim of robbery, hence he was not able to liquidate the said advances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant coordinate with lending institutions (LBP – Lending Center and DOF – MDFO) for the reconciliation of the loan balances and effect the appropriate adjustments. 

We also recommend securing certification from these institutions on the full payment of the accounts to support for the necessary adjustments. Henceforth, perform periodic reconciliation of balances of the Loans Payable-Domestic with the respective lending banks/institutions to reflect the accurate year-end balances.
observations.managementAction:
Reconciliation of Loans Payable - Domestic balances per book and per Lending Institutions is now ongoing. 

Balances are being reconciled by working back from present balances to beginning balances of the Loan accounts.
observations.reason: Reconciliation with the other loan accounts with the bank already effected but still contained unreconciled balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to implement the recommendations contained in the SAO Malampaya Fund report and make representations with the Department of Agriculture for the liquidation of the fund transfer.
observations.managementAction:
Liquidation Report was submitted to the Department of Agriculture (DA), Regional Field Unit No. 3 on October 4, 2017 amounting to ₱9,790,185.00

Unexpended balance of ₱209,815.00 was refunded to Department of Agriculture on August 4, 2015 per D.A. O.R. No. 7967146.
observations.reason: The Municipal Agriculturist and the Municipal Accountants are still awaiting for the comment and audit by the Chief Accountant of the Department of Agriculture.

observations.recommendation: We further recommended that Management make certain that the Public Private Partnership entered by the LGU with EcoNest will commence in accordance with the Memorandum of Agreement executed by both parties.
observations.managementAction: The Management ensured that the commencement of operations of the Sanitary Landfill (SLF) would be in accordance with the MOA.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 8.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated prior year’s recommendations and the Management agreed to

(a) ensure that projects embodied in the GAD Plan and Budget do address specific gender issues; and 

(b) through the GFPS, continue its efforts in establishing the GAD database as basis for planning, budgeting and policy formulation in accordance with the mandates of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01, or Guidelines on the Localization of the Magna Carta of Women.
observations.managementAction: Management already created monitoring and evaluation team that will determine if policies, structures, systems and mechanisms were put in place to institutionalize the pursuit of gender equality and women’s empowerment, to give way in formulating programs and projects including formulation of GAD database as basis of establishing programs.
observations.reason: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to 

(a) ensure that the programmed PPAs included in the Local Investment Plan for Children address the needs of the children in the locality as well as ensure the full implementation of the said PPA’s.

We recommended and the Management agreed to adopt measures and strategies to remedy the low collection of income for economic enterprises and minimize the expenses in relation thereto in order to generate sustainable revenues.
observations.managementAction: The Management created a Task Force in charge in the strict implementation of policies pertaining to efficient recording and collection of fees in the market.
observations.reason: The updating of municipality’s Market Code is already at its third and final reading.

observations.managementAction:
Liquidation Report was submitted to the Department of Agriculture (DA), Regional Field Unit No. 3 on October 4, 2017 amounting to P9,790,185.00

Unexpended balance of P209,815.00 was refunded to Department of Agriculture on August 4, 2015 per D.A. O.R. No. 7967146
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 5

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that upon completion of the general revision of property assessments, management provide additional personnel to finish updating the Certified List of Taxpayers for the Accountant to take up in the books, the RPT/SET Receivable with the corresponding subsidiary ledgers (SL) to monitor tax collections and delinquencies.

We also recommended and the Management agreed to make representations with the Provincial Assessor’s Office to address the problems encountered on the newly installed Property Assessment and Tax Administration System (PATAS) and to finally link the system with the Municipal Treasurer’s Office.

We recommended and Management agreed that the OIC- Municipal Accountant (a) send demand letters to the concerned accountable officers for the liquidation of their accountabilities; (b) stop the practice of granting new cash advances without the liquidation of previous ones; and (c) require the concerned personnel to liquidate their cash advances as soon as the purpose thereof had been accomplished.

We reiterated the following recommendations and the Management agreed that:
Inventory Committee to conduct an annual comprehensive/complete physical inventory of PPE by description type and  submit a report thereon to the Office of the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year;
OIC-GSO to take the necessary action to identify all unserviceable government properties and prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) so that proper disposal could be undertaken and the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting entry for properties already disposed.
Municipal Accountant and GSO to cause the preparation and maintenance of complete and updated PPE/Real Property Ledger Cards/ Property Cards to enable reconciliation with the general ledger balances and the inventory report, and monitor improvement and disposition of each property.
Prepare subsidiary ledgers for the Land account with complete details, reconcile the inventory list of the land from the accounting and the treasury; and provide budget and cause the transfer of land titles of all land acquired through donation and annotation of land without certificate of title to the Registry of Deeds.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant coordinate with lending institutions (LBP – Lending Center and DOF – MDFO) for the reconciliation of the loan balances and effect the appropriate adjustments. We also recommend securing certification from these institutions on the full payment of the accounts to support for the necessary adjustments. Henceforth, perform periodic reconciliation of balances of the Loans Payable-Domestic with the respective lending banks/institutions to reflect the accurate year-end balances.

We recommended and the Management agreed to implement the recommendations contained in the SAO Malampaya Fund report and make representations with the Department of Agriculture for the liquidation of the fund transfer.

observations.managementAction:
Cable is already installed
 Coordinated with the Provincial Assessor’s Office/ Geodata Officer

Updated Certified List of Taxpayers has been completed and to be submitted on December 27, 2017 to Office of the Municipal Accountant
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 1

observations.managementAction:
Executive Order No. 40 (Series of 2017) on the reorganization of the Inventory Team of LGU Hermosa was issued on November 13, 2017

Conduct of physical inventory is still ongoing
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 3

observations.managementAction:
Cash advance of Eng’r. Nestor Santos was already liquidated in 2017 and cash advance of Municipal Agriculturist Tomas Sotto was returned to Treasury Office on August 22, 2017 per O.R. No. 3961453

Efforts to collect the unliquidated cash advances of DepED personnel were futile because some of them have retired from the service and moved to other countries and one passed away.

We are seeking for the recommendation from The Commission on Audit if these cash advances can be written-off due to aging. There were advances made in the year 1982.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 2

observations.managementAction:
Audit Recommendations were followed but full compliance is expected to be undertaken in CY 2018

Members, Secretariat and Technical Working Group of BAC attended seminar in Baguio City to refresh their knowledge about Republic Act 9184 and its new Implementing Rules and Regulations.

All recommendations are already complied and implemented.
Municipal Planning and Development Council (MPDC) will convene on the 1st Quarter of 2018 for the implementation of 20% Development Fund
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 9

observations.managementAction:
Contractors are already complying with the Department of Labor and Employment (DOLE) Order No. 13.

Compliance is ensured thru inspections of the Office of the Municipal Engineer

Documents checklist is attached in every disbursement/ transaction.

The MDRRM Officer was appointed starting June 2017.

Preparation and submission to the Commission of monthly report on Sources and Utilization of MDRRM Fund will be complied and  implemented as recommended
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 10

observations.managementAction:
Unliquidated cash advance of Municipal Agriculturist Tomas Sotto was returned to Treasury Office on August 22, 2017 per O.R. No. 3961453

Liquidation Reports are ensured to be supported with complete documentary requirements

Hermosa Marshals are now duly deputized and actively enforcing Municipal Ordinances such as issuance of Traffic Citation Tickets to violators. They were trained by the PNP personnels and team-building activities were conducted in May 2017. Another team-building is scheduled in January 2018

Various meetings were being conducted by the Sangguniang Bayan Committee on Transportation to further enhance the policies and traffic rules.

Color coding if now fully implemented.

Sanggunian Resolution No. 2017-016, Municipal Ordinance 002, Series of 2017 known as the Standard Policy in Granting Financial Assistance was passed and approved on January 23, 2017 are is now being implemented.

10-Year Solid Waste Management Plan was submitted to the Environmental Management Board (EMB)

Municipal Sanitary Landfill is now operating thru the Public Private Partnership with the firm Econest to ensure compliance with RA 9003

Information campaign was conducted and strict implementation of no segregation, no collection policy is being implemented
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 6

observations.managementAction:
Community-Based Management System is now being implemented and GAD Database is already encoded and updated.

Geo-tagging of 8 Barangays was already finished and tagging of the remaining 15 Barangays will be continued in 2018

The Gender equality and women’s empowerment is spearheaded by our Municipal Social Welfare Officer as designated GAD Focal Person.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 7

Reiterated in Observation No. 10 Management did not establish the appropriate selection criteria of beneficiaries to ensure that the most qualified beneficiaries are recipients of the program. There were also no assessment/evaluation reports on the successful implementation of the program, consequently, the grantor and the implementing agency were not assured if the objectives identified in the BUB projects were met. Also, because of the delayed/non-implementation of the remaining projects for the Managerial Entrepreneurship and Product Development Consultancy, Management was not be given additional funding from the Department of Trade and Industry unless the unliquidated cash advance is refunded.

Reiterated in Observation No. 3 Management’s inaction on the prior years unliquidated cash advances and absence of strict adherence in the granting and liquidation of cash advances remain unliquidated or not refunded despite completion of the purpose for which these were granted.

observations.managementAction: Incomplete conduct of inventory made it not possible to reconcile general ledger balances with the inventory report.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 2
Appropriate adjustments were not yet effected on the reconciliation of the loan balances.

Reiterated in Observation No. 12 The policies/guidelines on the grant of donations and financial assistance of the Indigency Program or Assistance to Individuals in Crisis Situations (AICS) and to other government agencies, organizations and individuals approved by the Sanggunian Bayan was not yet implemented as of December 2016 resulting in varying amounts given to recipients.
Copies of approved contracts and purchase orders were still not submitted within five days upon execution or issuance.

observations.managementAction: Owing to voluminous transactions, the set up of special accounts for the economic enterprises in accordance with the Manual on the NGAS, Volume I to facilitate the audit and verification of the transactions incurred therefor was not yet prioritized by the Accounting Department.

observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 3 Management’s inaction on the prior years unliquidated cash advances and absence of strict adherence in the granting and liquidation of cash advances remain unliquidated or not refunded despite completion of the purpose for which these were granted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management provide
personnel to help the Treasurer update the Certified List of Taxpayers and submit it to the
Accountant.
1.8 We recommended that the Management take advantage of the newly installed
Property Assessment and Tax Administration System (PATAS) and find ways if it is
possible to link the system with the Municipal Treasurer’s Office.
1.9 We also recommended that Management comply with Section 20 of the NGAS
Manual on the setting up of Real Property Tax/Special Education Tax Receivable.
The Treasurer upon completion of the Certified List of Taxpayers should be
submitted to the Accountant. Based on this, the Accountant should take up in the
books, the RPT/SET Receivable with the corresponding subsidiary ledgers (SL) that
will monitor tax collections and delinquencies.

2.2 We recommended that Management:
a. Create an Inventory Committee that will conduct an annual
comprehensive/complete physical inventory of property, plant and
equipment and submit a report thereon to the Office of the Auditor not
later than January 31 of each year;
b. OIC-GSO take the necessary action to identify all unserviceable
government properties and prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of
Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) so that proper disposal could be
undertaken;
c. Municipal Accountant (i) make the necessary adjusting entry for
properties that were already disposed; and (ii) prepare subsidiary ledgers
for the Land account with complete details with records; and
d. Municipal Accountant and GSO cause the preparation and maintenance of
complete and updated PPE/Real Property Ledger Cards/ Property Cards
to enable reconciliation with the general ledger balances and the inventory
report, and monitor improvement and disposition of each property; and
e. Municipal Treasurer supply the complete details in the Inventory List of
Land to reconcile with the accounting records.

4.6 We also recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting
entries for the corresponding Due to NGAs amounting to ₱10,000,000.00 in its books
of accounts pending the liquidation of the said fund.

7.20 We recommended that the (a) Municipal Development Council comply with
the mandates of DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) 2011-1 and Annex
A of IRR of RA No. 9184 for the efficient planning, implementation and monitoring
of PPAs funded under the 20% Development Fund; (b) Municipal Accountant
maintain special accounts under the General Fund for development projects for
proper accountability and control of the use thereof to purposes other than the
fund’s intention; and (c) Municipal Budget Officer appropriate the projects covered
under the 20% development fund in accordance with Section 3 of DILG-DBM JMC.

8.4 We also recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) ensure
strict compliance with DOLE DO No. 13, Series of 1998 on the submission of
construction safety and health program approved by DOLE for the projects.
8.5 We further recommend that Management (i) provide clearly the effectivity of
the contract in the contract agreement for their succeeding projects; and (ii) comply
with COA Circular No. 2013-004 pertaining to Information and Publicity of
Program/Projects/Activities of Government Agencies so that policy on full disclosure
is adopted.

9.7 We also recommended that the (a) Municipal Accountant transfer to the
Trust Fund the unutilized balance of the LDRRMF so that that the Liabilities and
Government Equity under the General Fund and Trust Fund be properly presented
in the financial statements; and (b) OIC-MDRRMO to (i) prepare a certified
monthly Report on the Utilization of DRRMF and submit to COA on or before the
15th day after the end of each month.

10.11 We recommended that Management establish appropriate selection criteria of
beneficiaries for each project to ensure that the recipients are the most qualified and
committed beneficiaries in order not to waste government meager resources and
attain project objectives.
10.12 We also recommended that Management require the Municipal Agriculturist
to immediately refund the unliquidated cash advances amounting to ₱163,094.00.
10.13 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that all
liquidation reports are supported with complete documentations pursuant to Section
4(6) of PD No. 1445.

12.9 We recommended that Management provide financial assistance in
accordance with the written policy/guideline passed by the Sangguniang Bayan and
in consonance with the mandate of Section 36 of RA No. 7160.
12.10 We also recommended that all assistance to NGO/POs comply with the
requirements enumerated in COA Circular No. 96-003.

13.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) through the Solid Waste
Management Board encourage active participation of barangay officials to support
the solid waste management program of the government by educating their
respective constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials
from compostable wastes; (b) take appropriate action to ensure that the existing
MRF of the three component barangays are fully functional and provide necessary
support in enjoining the remaining barangays to establish their own MRF; (c)
through the SB adopt an ordinance pertaining to policies and possible measures that
will ensure full compliance by the barangays with the provisions of RA No. 9003,
more importantly the requirements/conditions prerequisite for the implementation
of the controlled dumpsite; (d) coordinate with the Provincial Agriculturist and the
PENRO Officer for the proper implementation of the prohibition on the open
burning of solid wastes like hay stalks, dried leaves and other farm wastes; (e)
encourage farmers to do composting of farm waste to enrich the soil with natural
fertilizers; and (f) conduct proper information dissemination like conducting
seminars, production of posters/tarpaulins on the harmful effects to the health of the
public of smoke from burning of solid wastes and assert their authority in the
implementation of the ordinance prohibiting the burning of hay stalks and other
farm wastes.

14.7 We recommended that Management (a) create monitoring and evaluation
team that will determine if policies, structures, systems and mechanisms were put in
place to institutionalize the pursuit of gender equality and women’s empowerment;
(b) ensure that projects embodied in the GAD Plan and Budget do address specific
gender issues; and (c) through the GFPS, continue its efforts in establishing the GAD
database as basis for planning, budgeting and policy formulation in accordance with
the mandates of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01
or Guidelines on the Localization of the Magna Carta of Women.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer completed the update of the certified list of taxpayers with the corresponding amount due and collectible.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 1 The set-up of RPT/SET Receivable was still based on estimates because the certified list of taxpayers was only completed December 2016.

observations.managementAction: Procurements for medicines were already supported with justification letter in accordance with the Administrative Order.
observations.reason: Some requirements of the provisions of the IRR of RA No. 9184 were not yet faithfully adhered to by BAC.

observations.managementAction: There is already documentation on traffic management programs.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 8 Construction and Health Program for infrastructure projects were not yet submitted by the Contractors.

observations.managementAction: We commend the Municipal Mayor for complying with the creation of the GFPS and TWG.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 14 Non-completion of GAD Database and absence of monitoring and evaluation team denied the agency of relevant GAD information as bases for gender-responsive planning, budgeting, programing and policy formulation as well as the assessment of the outcomes of the GAD Programs if it has indeed addressed the specific gender issues.

observations.managementAction: LGU conducted orientation and meetings on barangays pertaining to Solid Waste Management and printing of tarpaulins for the no segregation, no collection policy.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 13 For CY 2016, the Municipality still has no existing ordinance on “no segregation, no collection” policy to encourage barangays to segregate their garbage/wastes. Actual inspection and validation by the Audit Team showed that the mandatory segregation of wastes at source was not actively participated by the households, institutions and commercial/business establishments.
